sections.heading: Brand & Contact Information
sections.content:
Brand name: Soleil Studio
Project type: New product development — first formulation project
Brand stage: New / pre-launch
Distribution: DTC (own website) + boutique retail
Target retail price: $38–45 USD

sections.heading: Product Concept
sections.content:
Product type: SPF 50 daily moisturizer — no white cast
Target consumer: Melanin-rich / deeper skin tones
Positioning: Premium, clean beauty SPF brand owning the melanin-rich skin space
Texture benchmark: Fenty Skin Hydra Vizor — gel-cream, lightweight hybrid
Container: 50mL pump bottle

sections.heading: Key Requirements Summary
sections.content:
• Hybrid UV filter system: avobenzone + supporting chemical filters + zinc oxide under 10%
• Excluded UV actives: No oxybenzone, no octinoxate
• Excluded ingredients: No silicones, no mineral oil, no synthetic fragrance
• Claims: SPF 50 broad-spectrum, no white cast on melanin-rich skin
• Regulatory classification: US OTC drug product — FDA sunscreen monograph

sections.heading: UV Filter Strategy
sections.content:
Target SPF: 50 (broad-spectrum)
Filter approach: Hybrid mineral/chemical system
Preferred actives: Avobenzone + octisalate + low-level zinc oxide (<10%)
Excluded actives: Oxybenzone, octinoxate (client hard requirement — clean beauty positioning and global market compliance)
White cast mitigation: Zinc oxide at sub-10% with chemical filter co-system; consider coated zinc particles for further white cast reduction

sections.heading: Texture & Skin Feel
sections.content:
Target texture: Gel-cream hybrid — lightweight, fast-absorbing, non-greasy
Benchmark: Fenty Skin Hydra Vizor SPF 30
Key performance requirement: Must not pill under makeup — critical for daily wear claim
Exclusions: No silicones (dimethicone, cyclomethicone), no mineral oil
Suggested texture-building approach: Hydroxyethyl cellulose, hyaluronic acid, glycerin-forward humectant system

sections.heading: Fragrance & Preservation
sections.content:
Fragrance: Fragrance-free (no synthetic fragrance per client requirement)
Preservation: Broad-spectrum preservative system compatible with OTC drug product requirements
Note: Preservative selection must be reviewed in context of FDA OTC monograph compliance

sections.heading: Development Notes
sections.content:
Target pH: 6.0–7.0 (compatible with skin and UV filter stability)
Stability testing: ICH-compliant accelerated stability required for OTC drug product
SPF testing: Required — in vivo SPF 50 testing per FDA protocol (FDA 2011 Final Rule)
Critical path item: SPF testing timeline typically 8–12 weeks — must be initiated early in development

sections.heading: US Regulatory Classification
sections.content:
Classification: OTC drug product — FDA sunscreen monograph (21 CFR Part 352 / 2019 Proposed Rule)
Required labeling: Drug Facts panel — mandatory format per FDA
Active ingredient disclosure: Required with concentration (e.g., Avobenzone 3%, Zinc Oxide 9%)
SPF claim: SPF 50 — requires in vivo testing per FDA 2011 Final Rule
Broad-spectrum claim: Requires passing Critical Wavelength test (≥370 nm)
MoCRA compliance: Required as an OTC drug product sold in the US

sections.heading: Key Regulatory Actions Required
sections.content:
1. Select UV actives from FDA-approved sunscreen monograph list — confirm avobenzone + zinc system is within permitted concentrations
2. Commission in vivo SPF 50 testing (FDA-accredited lab) — budget 8–12 weeks and $8,000–$15,000 for testing
3. Prepare Drug Facts panel per FDA format requirements
4. Safety substantiation dossier — required under MoCRA
5. If Canada launch planned: Register with Health Canada; bilingual label required; different active ingredient allowances apply

sections.heading: Clean Beauty Compliance
sections.content:
Client clean beauty commitments:
• No oxybenzone (also banned in Hawaii, Key West, Palau — avoidance provides market flexibility)
• No octinoxate (EU restricted; avoiding provides EU-compatible formulation option)
• No silicones, no mineral oil, no synthetic fragrance
Note: These exclusions are cosmetic positioning commitments — they must be documented in the formulation brief and verified at every formula revision

sections.heading: Volume & Timeline
sections.content:
Initial production quantity: 3,000–5,000 units
Expected scale: DTC + boutique retail; potential for broader retail in Year 2
Target launch: 9 months from project start
Brand experience: First formulation project — client will need guidance through process milestones

sections.heading: Indicative Project Milestones
sections.content:
Month 1–2: Formulation development — prototype iterations
Month 3: SPF testing commissioned (in vivo — FDA lab)
Month 4: Formula lock — stability study initiated
Month 5–6: Packaging compatibility, label development (Drug Facts panel)
Month 7: SPF test results received; adjust if needed
Month 8: Regulatory review, final stability check
Month 9: Commercial production — launch

sections.heading: Open Items for Commercial Team
sections.content:
• SPF testing cost and timeline to be scoped and communicated to client early — this is the key critical path item
• Client has not confirmed packaging supplier — coordination needed on 50mL pump bottle lead time
• MOQ and COGS estimate to be provided once formula is closer to lock
• Client is first-time brand — recommend assigning a client success contact to guide them through milestones
